key_drivers: Safe-haven USD demand remains firm: DXY was 100.052 at 11:37 GMT on TradingView, up 0.19% on the day.
Risk conditions remain defensive: Cboe VIX spot was 29.56 as of March 27, 2026, up 7.73% from a 27.44 previous close.
The Fed held rates at 3.50%-3.75% on March 18, while GDPNow remained 2.0% for Q1 as of March 23, implying stable growth rather than reacceleration.
The RBA hiked 25 bps to 4.10% on March 17, while the BoJ held around 0.75% on March 19 but explicitly signaled further tightening if its outlook is realized.
Official FRED prints remain mixed but somewhat stale: DFII10 was 2.02 on March 25 after 2.06 on March 24, HY OAS narrowed to 3.17 from 3.19, and 2s10s flattened to 0.46 from 0.49.

overnight_changes: Equity-index quotes embedded on the DXY page showed broad U.S. equity losses during the session.
VIX remained above 29, confirming a persistent risk-off regime rather than a one-off intraday spike.
USD strength and elevated volatility continue to pressure metals, equities, and pro-cyclical FX while supporting crude and the yen.

imminent: Iran / Strait of Hormuz headlines affecting oil and risk sentiment
background: Next ECB / RBA / BoJ meetings and guidance shifts
